content: Tea consumption has a long history in Japan and China, with medicinal benefits recognized for centuries.
Catechins, antioxidant flavonoids, are responsible for the health benefits of tea, including anticarcinogenic, antibacterial, and cholesterol-lowering effects.
Matcha green tea contains 137 times the catechins compared to traditional Chinese green tea, due to its finely powdered form.
A 2011 study found that matcha's smaller particle size (2.83 μm) allows for greater absorption of catechins compared to regular green tea leaves (average 20 μm).
Higher quality matcha has a finer particle size (5 to 10 μm), while lower quality matcha has larger particles (over 10 μm).
Adding soy bean and black pepper to matcha can enhance the bioavailability of EGCG, the main catechin in green tea.
